action.reasoning:
Observation: Starting fresh. AAV fitness prediction task with 28K train, 3K val, ~735 AA sequences. Mutations concentrated in positions 561-588. Spearman rho metric. Decision: Build a baseline using ESM2-150M with frozen backbone + MLP head on mean-pooled embeddings of just the mutated region (positions 560-590 area to keep compute low). Or just use the mutation region embeddings. Let me first try a fast approach: encode just the variable region (positions 561-588 + some context) with ESM2-35M, mean pool, regression head. Train a few epochs to validate.

Actually, since mutations are concentrated in 561-588, I can extract just that region and train. But ESM was pretrained on full sequences; better to embed full or just feed the region. Let me go with feeding just the region (28 AA) through ESM2-35M which is fast. With 28K seqs * 1 epoch * batch 64, this should be quick.

Let me start by inspecting data and writing baseline.
